name: Phase estimation
description: Given parameters $n \ge 1$ and $\phi \in [0, 1]$, this benchmark uses quantum phase estimation to estimate $\phi$ to $n$ bits of precision. The phase estimation algorithm is applied to the unitary $U = R_Z(2\pi \phi) = \left( \begin{smallmatrix} e^{-2\pi i \phi} & 0 \\ 0 & e^{2\pi i \phi} \end{smallmatrix} \right)$ with eigenvector $\ket{1}$. For each $j = 0, \dots, 2^n - 1$, the controlled-$U^j$ operation is implemented using a sequence of controlled rotation gates. The algorithm is run for 1,000 shots. The fidelity measures how closely the distribution of measured bitstrings matches the theoretical distribution. The mean squared error measures the average of squared differences between the true phase $\phi$ and the estimated phase $\hat{\phi}$ reconstructed from the measured bitstrings.

metrics.fidelity: The fidelity between the measured distribution of bitstrings and the theoretical distribution.
metrics.mean_squared_error: Average of squared differences between the true phase $\phi$ and the estimated phase $\hat{\phi}$.
